     The Numbers Business Report

Into the Woods (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $128,002,372Details
International Box Office $84,413,945
Worldwide Box Office $212,416,317
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $19,501,114
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,456,498
Total $28,957,612
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A modern twist on several of the Brothers Grimm fairy tales, intertwining the plots of a few choice stories and exploring the consequences of the characters’ wishes and quests, follwing the classic tales of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Jack and the Beanstalk, and Rapunzel—all tied together by an original story involving a baker and his wife, their wish to begin a family and their interaction with the witch who has put a curse on them.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$31,051,923 (24.3% of total gross)
Legs:4.12 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:60.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$56,200,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,440 opening theaters/2,833 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $184,494,804

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2014 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: December 26th, 2014 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
January 8th, 2015 (Wide) (Australia)
January 9th, 2015 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 16th, 2015 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
January 23rd, 2015 (Wide) (Spain)
Video Release: March 24th, 2015 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements, fantasy action and peril, and some suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2340, 9/17/2014)
Running Time: 124 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Motion Capture Performance, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Curses, Dysfunctional Family, Giants, Infertility, Prince/Princess, Talking Animals, Witches
Subgenre: Epic Musical
Source:Based on Musical or Opera
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Lucamar, Marc Platt Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A modern twist on several of the Brothers Grimm fairy tales, intertwining the plots of a few choice stories and exploring the consequences of the characters’ wishes and quests, follwing the classic tales of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Jack and the Beanstalk, and Rapunzel—all tied together by an original story involving a baker and his wife, their wish to begin a family and their interaction with the witch who has put a curse on them.
